message:
test(e2e-large,bd-2gypv): retrofit all 7 cass-subprocess sites to use f2r5t timeout wrapper

Bead coding_agent_session_search-2gypv (follow-up to f2r5t / bead
ibuuh.10.12). The wrapper landed in commit 6664b8ac; this commit is
what makes it actually DO anything in production — swaps every
`cargo_bin_cmd!("cass")...assert().success()` in
tests/e2e_large_dataset.rs for a call through
`spawn_with_timeout_or_diag`, so any future subprocess hang in this
suite produces a structured diagnostic dump (phase, pid, elapsed,
data_dir listing, stream tails) instead of leaving cargo-test
silently stalled.

Seven call sites retrofitted across five tests:
  1. index_large_single_session: index --full
  2. index_many_conversations: index --full
  3. search_large_result_set: index --full
  4. search_large_result_set: search --limit 1000
  5. memory_bounded_during_index: index --full
  6. incremental_index_on_large_base: index --full
  7. incremental_index_on_large_base: index (incremental)

Added a file-local `run_cass_with_timeout(label, args, data_dir, home,
codex_home, timeout)` helper that bundles the env-isolation this
suite uses with the wrapper call. Each site now explicitly declares
its timeout budget:
  - full index ops: 300s (matches the existing
    max_index_duration_ms=10min ceiling, comfortable headroom)
  - incremental index: 120s (expected much faster than full)
  - search: 30s (existing assertion already insisted on <5s)

Assertions are preserved — every site still asserts the subprocess
exited success, now with stdout+stderr surfaced in the failure
message rather than swallowed by assert_cmd's implicit panic-on-fail.
Site 3 (search_large_result_set) previously called
`search_output.get_output().stdout`; that now becomes the direct
`search_output.stdout` since `spawn_with_timeout_or_diag` returns
`std::process::Output`.

Import hygiene: swapped `cargo_bin_cmd` for the narrower `cargo_bin`
now that the macro is no longer invoked. Added `std::time::Duration`
and `util::timeout::spawn_with_timeout_or_diag`.

Verified end-to-end:
  CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/data/tmp/rch_target_cass_p3 \
  rch exec -- cargo test --test e2e_large_dataset search_large_result_set
  -> 1 passed in 9.37s  (exercises 2 retrofitted sites)

  CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/data/tmp/rch_target_cass_p3 \
  rch exec -- cargo test --test e2e_large_dataset memory_bounded_during_index
  -> 1 passed in 149s   (1 retrofitted site, 1000-msg corpus — stays
                          well under the 300s budget)

  util::timeout self-tests unchanged: 4/4 in 0.30s.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
